rationale: The citation is not just a list mention: the paper explicitly cites Maimaran and Simonson (2011) in the theory section to explain why all-average and compromise options can be chosen as safer, criticism-avoiding choices. In Study 3, the authors directly use that prior finding to motivate an alternative-mechanism test (need for justification) and report null support for that account in their data. This gives the cited work a clear functional role in conceptual framing and mechanism discrimination.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Maimaran and Simonson (2011) as prior evidence that concern about criticism/justifiability can drive compromise and all-average choices under preference uncertainty. It then treats that pathway as an alternative account and tests need for justification, finding its own self-confidence mechanism explains the effect instead.
